findings.evidence: The proof only uses `AlgHom.convOne_def` and `AlgHom.comp_convMul_distrib`; Mathlib states the latter under `[Bialgebra R C]`, not `[HopfAlgebra R C]`, at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Convolution.lean:75`.
findings.fix: Move these declarations to a separate bialgebra section with `[Semiring H] [Bialgebra R H] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] ...`; the Hopf section can then reuse the same monoid hom as a group hom when the group instances are available.
findings.issue: `mapValue`, `mapValue_apply`, `mapValue_id`, and `mapValue_comp` are in the `Hopf` section, but preservation of convolution one/mul does not use the antipode or the group instance.

summary: The group instance is at the expected level, but the value-functoriality API is stated with an unnecessary Hopf assumption instead of the existing bialgebra-level convolution structure.
